justification: Both terminology mismatches identified (session/token and suspended/locked). terminology_alignment section explicitly maps both pairs with clear definitions and origin notes. Brief uses consistent terminology throughout with guidance on which term to use where. Both ACs preserved with exact error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED. Architecture details included: Redis TTL 1800, last_accessed_at field, token issuance check for suspended accounts.

observations.text: terminology_alignment section maps both pairs explicitly with actionable guidance — use 'token' in code, 'session' in user-facing messages; use 'suspended' as canonical status value
contradictions_resolved section separately flags the suspended/locked mismatch and resolves in Bird's favor as domain authority, giving Shaq clear provenance
Both ACs preserved in Given/When/Then format with exact error codes SESSION_EXPIRED (401) and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED (403)
Architecture details faithfully carried: Redis TTL 1800s, last_accessed_at field, sliding window reset, status check at token issuance
Low-confidence area explicitly flags that 'locked' may have been intentional rather than drift — Shaq knows this was an assumption, not a confirmed equivalence
Open questions surface a genuine gap (mid-session suspension) that neither source agent addressed, demonstrating synthesis beyond simple merge

terminology_alignment for suspended/locked maps domain_term to 'account status field' rather than directly to 'locked' — slightly obscures that MJ specifically used the word 'locked', though the definition text does clarify this
